title: context-current-state hardcodes framework-gaps/tasks/phase — inert on custom-vocabulary substrates
description: currentState() (context-sdk.ts ~682-825), behind the context-current-state op, derives focus / in-flight / ranked atomic-next / blocked from HARDCODED stock kinds and couplings: block kinds tasks / framework-gaps / phase; the blocked-by relation_type task_depends_on_task; a literal priority rank P0/P1/P2/P3; and the kind strings framework-gap / task in nextActions. status->bucket IS already config-driven (status-vocab resolveStatusVocabulary + config.status_buckets), but the KIND / field / ranking / relation couplings are literal. On a substrate whose state-bearing kinds are not tasks/framework-gaps the op returns empty/inert — confirmed: against .context-jit-spec-v2 (axioms/concepts/dispatch-modes) currentState returns an empty result (no active focus, empty inFlight/nextActions/blocked). No config registry declares which kinds are focus-eligible / next-ranked / blocked-checked, which field gives order/ranking, or which relation_type is blocked-by.

evidence.reference: hardcoded priorityRank P0/P1/P2/P3; ranking field hardcoded to priority

impact: where-are-we/what-next is a privilege of the stock PM vocabulary; a custom-vocabulary substrate with a real focus/next-state gets a silent empty result, not its own derivation. The op cannot serve any substrate that does not use framework-gaps + tasks.
proposed_resolution: Add a config-declared state-derivation registry (which kinds are focus-eligible / next-ranked / blocked-checked, the ranking field, the blocked-by relation_type, field+values -> open/blocked/done leveraging the existing status_buckets); rewire currentState() to read it instead of hardcoded literals; the shipped conception declares framework-gaps + tasks so stock behavior is byte-preserved; with no declaration return a truthful not-configured signal (not silent empty). Advances FEAT-004.
